name: Woody Herman
abstract: Woodrow Charles Herman (May 16, 1913 – October 29, 1987) was an American jazz clarinetist, saxophonist, singer, and big band leader. Leading groups called "The Herd", Herman came to prominence in the late 1930s and was active until his death in 1987.

works.t: Strike Up the Band: The Man I Love

quotes.text: And -- but basically, it was his music, as it should have been. And we learned to play the music, and presented it at Carnegie Hall in 1946, at our first jazz concert. And the critique was quite broad, some of it with great gusto and enjoyment and some of it couldn't understand what it was about and so consequently I would say it would remind me of some of the things that still happen.
quotes.caption: Woody Herman's first jazz concert at Carnegie Hall in 1946

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: I joined the band in Bridgeport, Connecticut... Woody Herman was like, wow, I can’t even begin to speak about what an influence he was... just being able to be on the bandstand with him really was an honor and a privilege

about_quotes.speaker: Kidd Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I used to listen and Woody Herman, he used to come from the Left Bank... I would have to go to bed and listen... I would have my head like on one of the things and listen to Woody Herman’s band
about_quotes.doc_title: Kidd Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 24, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: She played drums so good when she was kid that she played with…[Woody Herman?] It was one of those bandleaders during that period wanted her to go on the road with the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...the following band leaders called it quits: Woody Herman... Was this something you guys noticed? [INTERVIEWEE] No I don’t think they called it quits...
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Donn Andre
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Woody Herman only in the context of Ronnie Zito's career ('worked with Woody Herman'), not as a direct associate of the interviewee.
about_quotes.doc_title: Donn Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 6, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Hardaway
about_quotes.evidence: I agreed to join that band... I started on that band on New Years of 1955... Woody Herman’s music was more what I had in mind. So I stayed with Woody.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Hardaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999
